title: QUIC RFC 9000 NEW_CONNECTION_ID Frame Codec Work Item
summary: Carve direct positive, negative, edge, deterministic fuzz, and BenchmarkDotNet Dry proof for NEW_CONNECTION_ID frame parsing and formatting without claiming broader connection-ID lifecycle behavior.
planned_changes:
- Add focused architecture, work-item, and verification artifacts for the NEW_CONNECTION_ID frame-codec proof.
- Link the involved RFC 9000 Section 19.15 codec requirements to the focused artifact set while preserving the broad RFC9000-0001 trace.
- Replace stale broad test references with active requirement-home tests for valid field layout, length boundaries, truncation rejection, and Retire Prior To ordering.
- Link deterministic fuzz coverage for representative valid frame shapes and truncation rejection.
- Add permanent BenchmarkDotNet coverage for NEW_CONNECTION_ID parsing and formatting in the existing frame-codec benchmark suite.
- Preserve focused verification evidence under `artifacts/verification/20260425-rfc9000-new-connection-id-frame-codec/`.
verification_plan: Run the focused RFC 9000 NEW_CONNECTION_ID requirement-home tests, run `QuicFrameCodecBenchmarks` with the Dry job, render/check the touched SpecTrace artifacts, verify reciprocal trace links for the focused artifact set, and preserve evidence under `artifacts/verification/20260425-rfc9000-new-connection-id-frame-codec/`.
out_of_scope: Retire Prior To orchestration beyond codec-local ordering.
Public API controls for connection-ID lifecycle.
completion_notes: Completed as a proof and benchmark topoff over the existing NEW_CONNECTION_ID frame codec; no production behavior changed.
